tagline: Simplified change data capture for MySQL in Databricks.
what_it_does: The integrated CDC pipeline for MySQL allows users to ingest change data directly into Databricks using a single pipeline, which combines both extraction and application stages. This approach eliminates the need for a separate ingestion gateway, simplifying the setup process. The pipeline supports scheduled execution and auto full refresh, enhancing data management capabilities.
why_it_matters: This update streamlines the data ingestion process from MySQL, reducing operational complexity and setup time. By consolidating the pipeline architecture, organizations can achieve faster data updates and better resource utilization, ultimately leading to improved decision-making and analytics capabilities.
plain_summary: The integrated CDC pipeline for MySQL allows users to ingest change data directly into Databricks using a single pipeline, which combines both extraction and application stages. This approach eliminates the need for a separate ingestion gateway, simplifying…

capabilities.title: Unified Pipeline Architecture
capabilities.desc: Runs both extraction and application in a single pipeline, eliminating the need for a separate ingestion gateway.

capabilities.title: Scheduled Execution
capabilities.desc: Supports triggered execution, allowing users to schedule data ingestion at regular intervals.

capabilities.title: Auto Full Refresh
capabilities.desc: Automatically supports full refreshes, a feature not available in the standard gateway-based architecture.

capabilities.title: Exactly-Once Semantics
capabilities.desc: Ensures data integrity with exactly-once processing during ingestion.

prerequisites: Workspace must be enabled for Unity Catalog.
User must have necessary privileges on the metastore and target catalog/schema.
MySQL source setup must be completed.
use_cases: Organizations needing simplified MySQL data ingestion.
Data teams requiring scheduled updates for analytics.
Use cases involving auto full refresh for data accuracy.
limitations: Continuous execution is not supported; only scheduled execution is available.
Serverless compute is not supported; classic compute is required.

steps.title: Create Unity Catalog Connection
steps.desc: Establish a connection to MySQL in Unity Catalog.

steps.title: Define Pipeline Resource
steps.desc: Create the integrated CDC pipeline resource using YAML or API.

steps.title: Schedule Pipeline Execution
steps.desc: Set up a Lakeflow Jobs task to trigger the pipeline on a schedule.

steps.title: Deploy Pipeline
steps.desc: Use Databricks CLI to deploy and run the pipeline.

steps.title: Monitor Pipeline Updates
steps.desc: Check for successful execution and manage any queued updates.

key_takeaway: The MySQL integrated CDC pipeline simplifies data ingestion, enabling efficient and reliable change data capture directly into Databricks.
